revision.comment: 加筆歓迎
revision.text:
'''柳本古墳群'''（やなぎもとこふんぐん）は、[[天理市]]柳本町に所在する[[古墳時代]]前期の古墳群である。[[大和古墳群]]と併せて'''大和・柳本古墳群'''とも言われる。その南に纏向古墳群が存在する。
== 概要 == 本古墳群は、[[大和古墳群]]と谷を隔てて南の丘陵の縁辺に営まれており、2基の巨大古墳とその周囲の中・小形前方後円墳からなっている。まず、行燈山古墳を主墳に大和天神山・アンド山（120メートル）・南アンド山（65メートル）の3古墳が従属している。また南に渋谷古墳を主墳に上ノ山（125メートル）・シウロウ塚（120メートル）の二つの古墳群と西の方に柳本大塚（94メートル）などの古墳が点在している。
大和・柳本古墳群に続く王墓は、奈良盆地の東南の地を離れ、盆地西北部の地に佐紀盾列古墳群（西群）として営まれる。王権が移動したと考えられている。
== 主な古墳 == ;前方後円墳 *黒塚古墳（127.5メートル） *行燈山古墳（242メートル、伝崇神天皇陵） *大和天神山古墳（113メートル） *渋谷向山古墳（300メートル、伝景行天皇陵） ;双方中円墳 *櫛山古墳（150メートル）
== 関連項目 == *[[大和古墳群]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:やなきもとこふんくん}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:日本の考古遺跡]] [[Category:古墳時代]] [[Category:奈良県の歴史]] [[Category:天理市]]

revision.comment: CGの地点変更
revision.text:
'''柳本古墳群'''（やなぎもとこふんぐん）は、[[天理市]]柳本町に所在する[[古墳時代]]前期の古墳群である。[[大和古墳群]]と併せて'''大和・柳本古墳群'''とも言われる。その南に[[纏向古墳群]]が存在する。
== 概要 == 本古墳群は、[[大和古墳群]]と谷を隔てて南の丘陵の縁辺に営まれており、2基の巨大古墳とその周囲の中・小形前方後円墳からなっている。まず、行燈山古墳を主墳に大和天神山・アンド山（120メートル）・南アンド山（65メートル）の3古墳が従属している。また南に渋谷古墳を主墳に上ノ山（125メートル）・シウロウ塚（120メートル）の二つの古墳群と西の方に柳本大塚（94メートル）などの古墳が点在している。
大和・柳本古墳群に続く王墓は、奈良盆地の東南の地を離れ、盆地西北部の地に佐紀盾列古墳群（西群）として営まれる。王権が移動したと考えられている。
== 主な古墳 == [[File:Andonyama 001.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|行燈山古墳}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}。}}]] [[File:CG S Mukaiyama Kofun 11 7p BW.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|渋谷向山古墳}}]] ;前方後円墳 *[[黒塚古墳]]（127.5メートル） *[[行燈山古墳]]（242メートル、伝崇神天皇陵） *[[大和天神山古墳]]（113メートル） *[[渋谷向山古墳]]（300メートル、伝景行天皇陵） ;双方中円墳 *[[櫛山古墳]]（150メートル）
== 関連項目 == *[[大和古墳群]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やなきもとこふんくん}} [[Category:柳本古墳群|*]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:古墳群]]

revision.text:
'''Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (柳本古墳群) is a [[Kofun period]] burial mound cluster located in [[Yanagimoto, Tenri]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. Together with the [[Yamato Kofun Group]], it is collectively referred to as the '''Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group'''. To its south lies the [[Makimuku Kofun Group]].
== Overview ==
This kofun group is situated on the southern ridge of the hill, separated by a valley from the [[Yamato Kofun Group]]. It consists of two giant burial mounds surrounded by medium- and small-sized keyhole-shaped kofun. The Andonyama Kofun serves as the primary burial mound, accompanied by three subsidiary kofun: {{ill|Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳}}, {{ill|Andoyama Kofun|ja|アンド山古墳}} (120 meters), and {{ill|Minami Andoyama Kofun|ja|南アンド山古墳}} (65 meters). To the south, the Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun is the primary mound in a group that includes the {{ill|Uenoyama Kofun (Tenri)|ja|上ノ山古墳}} (125 meters) and {{ill|Shurozuka Kofun|ja|シウロウ塚古墳}} (120 meters). Additionally, smaller burial mounds like the {{ill|Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|ja|柳本大塚古墳}} (94 meters) are scattered to the west.
Following the Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group, royal burial mounds shifted to the northwestern part of the Nara Basin, forming the [[Saki Kofun Cluster (West Group)|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群]] (Western Group). This transition is thought to indicate a movement of the royal power base.
== Major Kofun == [[File:Andonyama 001.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Andonyama Kofun}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}.}}]] [[File:CG S Mukaiyama Kofun 11 7p BW.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun}}]] [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|The relationship between the Yanagimoto Kofun Group and other kofun groups]] ;Keyhole-shaped kofun * {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}} (127.5 meters) * {{ill|Andonyama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳}} (242 meters, traditionally attributed to Emperor Sujin) * {{ill|Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳}} (113 meters) * {{ill|Shibuya Mukaiyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳}} (300 meters, traditionally attributed to Emperor Keikō) ;Square mound with a round top * {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}} (150 meters)
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Yanagimoto Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[ja:柳本古墳群]]

== Overview ==

'''Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (柳本古墳群, *Yanagimoto Kofungun*) is a group of early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} burial mounds located in Yanagimoto-chō, {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}. Together with the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}}, it is also referred to as the '''Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (大和・柳本古墳群). To the south lies the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纏向古墳群}}.
== Overview == This kofun group is situated on the southern ridges of hills, separated by a valley from the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}}, and comprises two large kofun and several medium- and small-sized keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳). The main mound, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳}}, is accompanied by three subordinate tumuli: {{ill|Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳}}, {{ill|Ando-yama Kofun (Inner Tomb)|ja|アンド山古墳}} (120 meters), and {{ill|South Ando-yama Kofun|ja|南アンド山古墳}} (65 meters). To the south, another group centers on {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳}} and includes the {{ill|Kamiyama Kofun|ja|上ノ山古墳}} (125 meters) and {{ill|Shiurou Kofun|ja|シウロウ塚古墳}} (120 meters). To the west, tumuli such as {{ill|Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|ja|柳本大塚古墳}} (94 meters) are scattered.
The royal tombs following the Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group shifted from the southeastern part of the Nara Basin to the northwest, forming the {{ill|Saki Tsurugamine Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} (western group). This shift is thought to reflect the relocation of royal power.
== Notable Kofun == [[File:Andonyama 001.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Andon-yama Kofun}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}.}}]] [[File:CG S Mukaiyama Kofun 11 7p BW.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}}]] [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|Location of the Yanagimoto Kofun Group and nearby kofun groups]]
; Keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳) * {{ill|Kurotsuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}} (127.5 meters) * {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳}} (242 meters, attributed to Emperor Sujin) * {{ill|Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳}} (113 meters) * {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳}} (300 meters, attributed to Emperor Keikō)
; Square-shaped tumuli (双方中円墳) * {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}} (150 meters)
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Yanagimoto Kofun Group|*]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Kofun groups]] [[ja:柳本古墳群]]

'''Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (柳本古墳群, *Yanagimoto Kofungun*) is a group of early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} burial mounds located in Yanagimoto-chō, {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}. Together with the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}}, it is also referred to as the '''Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (大和・柳本古墳群). To the south lies the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纏向古墳群}}.

To the west, tumuli such as {{ill|Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|ja|柳本大塚古墳}} (94 meters) are scattered.

To the south, another group centers on {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳}} and includes the {{ill|Kamiyama Kofun|ja|上ノ山古墳}} (125 meters) and {{ill|Shiurou Kofun|ja|シウロウ塚古墳}} (120 meters). To the west, tumuli such as {{ill|Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|ja|柳本大塚古墳}} (94 meters) are scattered.
The royal tombs following the Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group shifted from the southeastern part of the Nara Basin to the northwest, forming the {{ill|Saki Tsurugamine Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} (western group). This shift is thought to reflect the relocation of royal power.

'''Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (柳本古墳群, *Yanagimoto Kofungun*) is a group of early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} burial mounds located in Yanagimoto-chō, {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}. Together with the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}}, it is also referred to as the '''Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (大和・柳本古墳群). To the south lies the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纏向古墳群}}.
== Overview == This kofun group is situated on the southern ridges of hills, separated by a valley from the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}}, and comprises two large kofun and several medium- and small-sized keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳). The main mound, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳}}, is accompanied by three subordinate tumuli: {{ill|Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳}}, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳}} (120 meters), and {{ill|South Ando-yama Kofun|ja|南アンド山古墳}} (65 meters). To the south, another group centers on {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳}} and includes the {{ill|Kamiyama Kofun|ja|上ノ山古墳}} (125 meters) and {{ill|Shiurou Kofun|ja|シウロウ塚古墳}} (120 meters). To the west, tumuli such as {{ill|Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|ja|柳本大塚古墳}} (94 meters) are scattered.
The royal tombs following the Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group shifted from the southeastern part of the Nara Basin to the northwest, forming the {{ill|Saki Tsurugamine Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} (western group). This shift is thought to reflect the relocation of royal power.
== Notable Kofun == [[File:Andonyama 001.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Andon-yama Kofun}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}.}}]] [[File:CG S Mukaiyama Kofun 11 7p BW.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}}]] [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|Location of the Yanagimoto Kofun Group and nearby kofun groups]]
; Keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳) * {{ill|Kurotsuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}} (127.5 meters) * {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳}} (242 meters, attributed to Emperor Sujin) * {{ill|Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳}} (113 meters) * {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳}} (300 meters, attributed to Emperor Keikō)
; Square-shaped tumuli (双方中円墳) * {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}} (150 meters)
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}}

revision.text:
'''Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (柳本古墳群, *Yanagimoto Kofungun*) is a group of early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} burial mounds located in Yanagimoto-chō, {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}. Together with the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}}, it is also referred to as the '''Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (大和・柳本古墳群). To the south lies the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纏向古墳群}}.
== Overview == This kofun group is situated on the southern ridges of hills, separated by a valley from the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}}, and comprises two large kofun and several medium- and small-sized keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳). The main mound, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳}}, is accompanied by three subordinate tumuli: {{ill|Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳}}, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳}} (120 meters), and {{ill|South Ando-yama Kofun|ja|南アンド山古墳}} (65 meters). To the south, another group centers on {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳}} and includes the {{ill|Kamiyama Kofun|ja|上ノ山古墳}} (125 meters) and {{ill|Shiurou Kofun|ja|シウロウ塚古墳}} (120 meters). To the west, tumuli such as {{ill|Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|ja|柳本大塚古墳}} (94 meters) are scattered.
The royal tombs following the Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group shifted from the southeastern part of the Nara Basin to the northwest, forming the {{ill|Saki Tsurugamine Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} (western group). This shift is thought to reflect the relocation of royal power.
== Notable Kofun == [[File:Andonyama 001.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Andon-yama Kofun}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}.}}]] [[File:CG S Mukaiyama Kofun 11 7p BW.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}}]] [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|Location of the Yanagimoto Kofun Group and nearby kofun groups]]

The main mound, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳}}, is accompanied by three subordinate tumuli: {{ill|Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳}}, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳}} (120 meters), and {{ill|South Ando-yama Kofun|ja|南アンド山古墳}} (65 meters). To the south, another group centers on {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳}} and includes the {{ill|Kamiyama Kofun|ja|上ノ山古墳}} (125 meters) and {{ill|Shiurou Kofun|ja|シウロウ塚古墳}} (120 meters). To the west, tumuli such as {{ill|Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|ja|柳本大塚古墳}} (94 meters) are scattered.
The royal tombs following the Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group shifted from the southeastern part of the Nara Basin to the northwest, forming the {{ill|Saki Tsurugamine Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} (western group). This shift is thought to reflect the relocation of royal power.

revision.text:
'''Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (柳本古墳群, *Yanagimoto Kofungun*) is a group of early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} burial mounds located in Yanagimoto-chō, {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}. Together with the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}}, it is also referred to as the '''Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (大和・柳本古墳群). To the south lies the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纏向古墳群}}.
== Overview == This kofun group is situated on the southern ridges of hills, separated by a valley from the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}}, and comprises two large kofun and several medium- and small-sized keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳). The main mound, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳}}, is accompanied by three subordinate tumuli: {{ill|Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳}}, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳}} (120 meters), and {{ill|South Ando-yama Kofun|ja|南アンド山古墳}} (65 meters). To the south, another group centers on {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳}} and includes the {{ill|Kamiyama Kofun|ja|上ノ山古墳}} (125 meters) and {{ill|Shiurou Kofun|ja|シウロウ塚古墳}} (120 meters). To the west, tumuli such as {{ill|Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|ja|柳本大塚古墳}} (94 meters) are scattered.
The royal tombs following the Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group shifted from the southeastern part of the Nara Basin to the northwest, forming the {{ill|Saki Tsurugamine Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} (western group). This shift is thought to reflect the relocation of royal power.

revision.comment: added [[Category:Qq]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster]] using [[Help:Gadget-HotCat|HotCat]]
revision.text:
'''Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (柳本古墳群, *Yanagimoto Kofungun*) is a group of early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} burial mounds located in Yanagimoto-chō, {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}. Together with the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}}, it is also referred to as the '''Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (大和・柳本古墳群). To the south lies the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纏向古墳群}}.
== Overview == This kofun group is situated on the southern ridges of hills, separated by a valley from the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}}, and comprises two large kofun and several medium- and small-sized keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳). The main mound, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳}}, is accompanied by three subordinate tumuli: {{ill|Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳}}, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳}} (120 meters), and {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|南アンド山古墳|Andon-yama Kofun=Andon-yama Kofun|lt=South Ando-yama Kofun}} (65 meters). To the south, another group centers on {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳}} and includes the {{ill|Kamiyama Kofun|ja|上ノ山古墳}} (125 meters) and {{ill|Shiurou Kofun|ja|シウロウ塚古墳}} (120 meters). To the west, tumuli such as {{ill|Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|ja|柳本大塚古墳}} (94 meters) are scattered.
The royal tombs following the Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group shifted from the southeastern part of the Nara Basin to the northwest, forming the {{ill|Saki Tsurugamine Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} (western group). This shift is thought to reflect the relocation of royal power.
== Notable Kofun == [[File:Andonyama 001.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Andon-yama Kofun}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}.}}]] [[File:CG S Mukaiyama Kofun 11 7p BW.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}}]] [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|Location of the Yanagimoto Kofun Group and nearby kofun groups]]
; Keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳) * {{ill|Kurotsuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}} (127.5 meters) * {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳}} (242 meters, attributed to Emperor Sujin) * {{ill|Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳}} (113 meters) * {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳}} (300 meters, attributed to Emperor Keikō)
; Square-shaped tumuli (双方中円墳) * {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}} (150 meters)
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}}

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Subst:MAINSPACE]] to [[Category:qq]][[en:{{subst:PAGENAME}}}][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (柳本古墳群, *Yanagimoto Kofungun*) is a group of early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} burial mounds located in Yanagimoto-chō, {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}. Together with the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}}, it is also referred to as the '''Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (大和・柳本古墳群). To the south lies the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纏向古墳群}}.
== Overview == This kofun group is situated on the southern ridges of hills, separated by a valley from the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}}, and comprises two large kofun and several medium- and small-sized keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳). The main mound, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳}}, is accompanied by three subordinate tumuli: {{ill|Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳}}, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳}} (120 meters), and {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|南アンド山古墳|Andon-yama Kofun=Andon-yama Kofun|lt=South Ando-yama Kofun}} (65 meters). To the south, another group centers on {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳}} and includes the {{ill|Kamiyama Kofun|ja|上ノ山古墳}} (125 meters) and {{ill|Shiurou Kofun|ja|シウロウ塚古墳}} (120 meters). To the west, tumuli such as {{ill|Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|ja|柳本大塚古墳}} (94 meters) are scattered.
The royal tombs following the Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group shifted from the southeastern part of the Nara Basin to the northwest, forming the {{ill|Saki Tsurugamine Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} (western group). This shift is thought to reflect the relocation of royal power.
== Notable Kofun == [[File:Andonyama 001.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Andon-yama Kofun}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}.}}]] [[File:CG S Mukaiyama Kofun 11 7p BW.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}}]] [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|Location of the Yanagimoto Kofun Group and nearby kofun groups]]
; Keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳) * {{ill|Kurotsuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}} (127.5 meters) * {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳}} (242 meters, attributed to Emperor Sujin) * {{ill|Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳}} (113 meters) * {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳}} (300 meters, attributed to Emperor Keikō)
; Square-shaped tumuli (双方中円墳) * {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}} (150 meters)
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}}

To the south, another group centers on {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳}} and includes the {{ill|Kamiyama Kofun|ja|上ノ山古墳}} (125 meters) and {{ill|Shiurou Kofun|ja|シウロウ塚古墳}} (120 meters). To the west, tumuli such as {{ill|Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|ja|柳本大塚古墳}} (94 meters) are scattered.
The royal tombs following the Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group shifted from the southeastern part of the Nara Basin to the northwest, forming the {{ill|Saki Tsurugamine Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} (western group). This shift is thought to reflect the relocation of royal power.
== Notable Kofun == [[File:Andonyama 001.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Andon-yama Kofun}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}.}]]] [[File:CG S Mukaiyama Kofun 11 7p BW.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}]]] [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|Location of the Yanagimoto Kofun Group and nearby kofun groups]]
; Keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳) * {{ill|Kurotsuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}} (127.5 meters) * {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳}} (242 meters, attributed to Emperor Sujin) * {{ill|Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳}} (113 meters) * {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳}} (300 meters, attributed to Emperor Keikō)

== Overview == This kofun group is situated on the southern ridges of hills, separated by a valley from the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}}, and comprises two large kofun and several medium- and small-sized keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳). The main mound, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳}}, is accompanied by three subordinate tumuli: {{ill|Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳}}, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳}} (120 meters), and {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|南アンド山古墳|Andon-yama Kofun=Andon-yama Kofun|lt=South Ando-yama Kofun}} (65 meters). To the south, another group centers on {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳}} and includes the {{ill|Kamiyama Kofun|ja|上ノ山古墳}} (125 meters) and {{ill|Shiurou Kofun|ja|シウロウ塚古墳}} (120 meters). To the west, tumuli such as {{ill|Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|ja|柳本大塚古墳}} (94 meters) are scattered.
The royal tombs following the Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group shifted from the southeastern part of the Nara Basin to the northwest, forming the {{ill|Saki Tsurugamine Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} (western group). This shift is thought to reflect the relocation of royal power.

'''Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (柳本古墳群, *Yanagimoto Kofungun*) is a group of early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} burial mounds located in Yanagimoto-chō, {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}. Together with the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}}, it is also referred to as the '''Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (大和・柳本古墳群). To the south lies the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纏向古墳群}}.
== Overview == This kofun group is situated on the southern ridges of hills, separated by a valley from the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}}, and comprises two large kofun and several medium- and small-sized keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳). The main mound, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳}}, is accompanied by three subordinate tumuli: {{ill|Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳}}, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳}} (120 meters), and {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|南アンド山古墳|Andon-yama Kofun=Andon-yama Kofun|lt=South Ando-yama Kofun}} (65 meters). To the south, another group centers on {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳}} and includes the {{ill|Kamiyama Kofun|ja|上ノ山古墳}} (125 meters) and {{ill|Shiurou Kofun|ja|シウロウ塚古墳}} (120 meters). To the west, tumuli such as {{ill|Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|ja|柳本大塚古墳}} (94 meters) are scattered.
The royal tombs following the Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group shifted from the southeastern part of the Nara Basin to the northwest, forming the {{ill|Saki Tsurugamine Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} (western group). This shift is thought to reflect the relocation of royal power.
== Notable Kofun == [[File:Andonyama 001.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Andon-yama Kofun}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}.}]]] [[File:CG S Mukaiyama Kofun 11 7p BW.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}]]] [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|Location of the Yanagimoto Kofun Group and nearby kofun groups]]
; Keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳) * {{ill|Kurotsuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}} (127.5 meters) * {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳}} (242 meters, attributed to Emperor Sujin) * {{ill|Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳}} (113 meters) * {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳}} (300 meters, attributed to Emperor Keikō)
; Square-shaped tumuli (双方中円墳) * {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}} (150 meters)
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}}

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (柳本古墳群, *Yanagimoto Kofungun*) is a group of early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} burial mounds located in Yanagimoto-chō, {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}. Together with the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}}, it is also referred to as the '''Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (大和・柳本古墳群). To the south lies the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纏向古墳群}}.
== Overview == This kofun group is situated on the southern ridges of hills, separated by a valley from the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}}, and comprises two large kofun and several medium- and small-sized keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳). The main mound, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳}}, is accompanied by three subordinate tumuli: {{ill|Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳}}, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳}} (120 meters), and {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|南アンド山古墳|Andon-yama Kofun=Andon-yama Kofun|lt=South Ando-yama Kofun}} (65 meters). To the south, another group centers on {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳}} and includes the {{ill|Kamiyama Kofun|ja|上ノ山古墳}} (125 meters) and {{ill|Shiurou Kofun|ja|シウロウ塚古墳}} (120 meters). To the west, tumuli such as {{ill|Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|ja|柳本大塚古墳}} (94 meters) are scattered.
The royal tombs following the Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group shifted from the southeastern part of the Nara Basin to the northwest, forming the {{ill|Saki Tsurugamine Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} (western group). This shift is thought to reflect the relocation of royal power.
== Notable Kofun == [[File:Andonyama 001.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Andon-yama Kofun}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}.}]]] [[File:CG S Mukaiyama Kofun 11 7p BW.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}]]] [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|Location of the Yanagimoto Kofun Group and nearby kofun groups]]
; Keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳) * {{ill|Kurotsuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}} (127.5 meters) * {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳}} (242 meters, attributed to Emperor Sujin) * {{ill|Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳}} (113 meters) * {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳}} (300 meters, attributed to Emperor Keikō)
; Square-shaped tumuli (双方中円墳) * {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}} (150 meters)
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}}

revision.comment: trimming these off
revision.text:
'''Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (柳本古墳群, *Yanagimoto Kofungun*) is a group of early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} burial mounds located in Yanagimoto-chō, {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}. Together with the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}}, it is also referred to as the '''Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (大和・柳本古墳群). To the south lies the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纏向古墳群}}.
== Overview == This kofun group is situated on the southern ridges of hills, separated by a valley from the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}}, and comprises two large kofun and several medium- and small-sized keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳). The main mound, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳}}, is accompanied by three subordinate tumuli: {{ill|Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳}}, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳}} (120 meters), and {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|南アンド山古墳|Andon-yama Kofun=Andon-yama Kofun|lt=South Ando-yama Kofun}} (65 meters). To the south, another group centers on {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳}} and includes the {{ill|Kamiyama Kofun|ja|上ノ山古墳}} (125 meters) and {{ill|Shiurou Kofun|ja|シウロウ塚古墳}} (120 meters). To the west, tumuli such as {{ill|Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|ja|柳本大塚古墳}} (94 meters) are scattered.
The royal tombs following the Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group shifted from the southeastern part of the Nara Basin to the northwest, forming the {{ill|Saki Tsurugamine Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} (western group). This shift is thought to reflect the relocation of royal power.
== Notable Kofun == [[File:Andonyama 001.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Andon-yama Kofun}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}.}]]] [[File:CG S Mukaiyama Kofun 11 7p BW.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}]]] [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|Location of the Yanagimoto Kofun Group and nearby kofun groups]]
; Keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳) * {{ill|Kurotsuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}} (127.5 meters) * {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳}} (242 meters, attributed to Emperor Sujin) * {{ill|Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳}} (113 meters) * {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳}} (300 meters, attributed to Emperor Keikō)
; Square-shaped tumuli (双方中円墳) * {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}} (150 meters)
== See Also == * {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}}

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Q--q]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (柳本古墳群, *Yanagimoto Kofungun*) is a group of early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} burial mounds located in Yanagimoto-chō, {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}. Together with the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}}, it is also referred to as the '''Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (大和・柳本古墳群). To the south lies the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纏向古墳群}}.
== Overview == This kofun group is situated on the southern ridges of hills, separated by a valley from the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}}, and comprises two large kofun and several medium- and small-sized keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳). The main mound, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳}}, is accompanied by three subordinate tumuli: {{ill|Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳}}, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳}} (120 meters), and {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|南アンド山古墳|Andon-yama Kofun=Andon-yama Kofun|lt=South Ando-yama Kofun}} (65 meters). To the south, another group centers on {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}} and includes the {{ill|Kamiyama Kofun|ja|上ノ山古墳}} (125 meters) and {{ill|Shiurou Kofun|ja|シウロウ塚古墳}} (120 meters). To the west, tumuli such as {{ill|Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|ja|柳本大塚古墳}} (94 meters) are scattered.
The royal tombs following the Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group shifted from the southeastern part of the Nara Basin to the northwest, forming the {{ill|Saki Tsurugamine Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} (western group). This shift is thought to reflect the relocation of royal power.
== Notable Kofun == [[File:Andonyama 001.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Andon-yama Kofun}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}.}]]] [[File:CG S Mukaiyama Kofun 11 7p BW.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}]]] [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|Location of the Yanagimoto Kofun Group and nearby kofun groups]]
; Keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳) * {{ill|Kurotsuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}} (127.5 meters) * {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳}} (242 meters, attributed to Emperor Sujin) * {{ill|Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳}} (113 meters) * {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}} (300 meters, attributed to Emperor Keikō)
; Square-shaped tumuli (双方中円墳) * {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}} (150 meters)
== See Also == * {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}}

To the south, another group centers on {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}} and includes the {{ill|Kamiyama Kofun|ja|上ノ山古墳}} (125 meters) and {{ill|Shiurou Kofun|ja|シウロウ塚古墳}} (120 meters). To the west, tumuli such as {{ill|Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|ja|柳本大塚古墳}} (94 meters) are scattered.
The royal tombs following the Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group shifted from the southeastern part of the Nara Basin to the northwest, forming the {{ill|Saki Tsurugamine Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} (western group). This shift is thought to reflect the relocation of royal power.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "qq" to "11"
revision.text:
'''Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (柳本古墳群, *Yanagimoto Kofungun*) is a group of early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} Kofuns located in Yanagimoto-chō, {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}. Together with the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}}, it is also referred to as the '''Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (大和・柳本古墳群). To the south lies the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纏向古墳群}}.
== Overview == This kofun group is situated on the southern ridges of hills, separated by a valley from the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}}, and comprises two large kofun and several medium- and small-sized keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳). The main mound, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳}}, is accompanied by three subordinate tumuli: {{ill|Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳}}, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳}} (120 meters), and {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|南アンド山古墳|Andon-yama Kofun=Andon-yama Kofun|lt=South Ando-yama Kofun}} (65 meters). To the south, another group centers on {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}} and includes the {{ill|Kamiyama Kofun|ja|上ノ山古墳}} (125 meters) and {{ill|Shiurou Kofun|ja|シウロウ塚古墳}} (120 meters). To the west, tumuli such as {{ill|Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|ja|柳本大塚古墳}} (94 meters) are scattered.
The royal tombs following the Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group shifted from the southeastern part of the Nara Basin to the northwest, forming the {{ill|Saki Tsurugamine Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} (western group). This shift is thought to reflect the relocation of royal power.
== Notable Kofun == [[File:Andonyama 001.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Andon-yama Kofun}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}.}]]] [[File:CG S Mukaiyama Kofun 11 7p BW.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}]]] [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|Location of the Yanagimoto Kofun Group and nearby kofun groups]]
; Keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳) * {{ill|Kurotsuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}} (127.5 meters) * {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳}} (242 meters, attributed to Emperor Sujin) * {{ill|Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳}} (113 meters) * {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}} (300 meters, attributed to Emperor Keikō)
; Square-shaped tumuli (双方中円墳) * {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}} (150 meters)
== See Also == * {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}}

To the south, another group centers on {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}} and includes the {{ill|Kamiyama Kofun|ja|上ノ山古墳}} (125 meters) and {{ill|Shiurou Kofun|ja|シウロウ塚古墳}} (120 meters). To the west, tumuli such as {{ill|Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|ja|柳本大塚古墳}} (94 meters) are scattered.
The royal tombs following the Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group shifted from the southeastern part of the Nara Basin to the northwest, forming the {{ill|Saki Tsurugamine Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} (western group). This shift is thought to reflect the relocation of royal power.

revision.comment: moving the kofun template to the bottom of each page
revision.text:
'''Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (柳本古墳群, *Yanagimoto Kofungun*) is a group of early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} Kofuns located in Yanagimoto-chō, {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}. Together with the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}}, it is also referred to as the '''Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (大和・柳本古墳群). To the south lies the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纏向古墳群}}.
== Overview == This kofun group is situated on the southern ridges of hills, separated by a valley from the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}}, and comprises two large kofun and several medium- and small-sized keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳). The main mound, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳}}, is accompanied by three subordinate tumuli: {{ill|Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳}}, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳}} (120 meters), and {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|南アンド山古墳|Andon-yama Kofun=Andon-yama Kofun|lt=South Ando-yama Kofun}} (65 meters). To the south, another group centers on {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}} and includes the {{ill|Kamiyama Kofun|ja|上ノ山古墳}} (125 meters) and {{ill|Shiurou Kofun|ja|シウロウ塚古墳}} (120 meters). To the west, tumuli such as {{ill|Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|ja|柳本大塚古墳}} (94 meters) are scattered.
The royal tombs following the Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group shifted from the southeastern part of the Nara Basin to the northwest, forming the {{ill|Saki Tsurugamine Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} (western group). This shift is thought to reflect the relocation of royal power.
== Notable Kofun == [[File:Andonyama 001.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Andon-yama Kofun}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}.}]]] [[File:CG S Mukaiyama Kofun 11 7p BW.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}]]] [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|Location of the Yanagimoto Kofun Group and nearby kofun groups]]
; Keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳) * {{ill|Kurotsuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}} (127.5 meters) * {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳}} (242 meters, attributed to Emperor Sujin) * {{ill|Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳}} (113 meters) * {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}} (300 meters, attributed to Emperor Keikō)
; Square-shaped tumuli (双方中円墳) * {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}} (150 meters)
== See Also == * {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}}

The main mound, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳}}, is accompanied by three subordinate tumuli: {{ill|Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳}}, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳}} (120 meters), and {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|南アンド山古墳|Andon-yama Kofun=Andon-yama Kofun|lt=South Ando-yama Kofun}} (65 meters). To the south, another group centers on {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}} and includes the {{ill|Kamiyama Kofun|ja|上ノ山古墳}} (125 meters) and {{ill|Shiurou Kofun|ja|シウロウ塚古墳}} (120 meters). To the west, tumuli such as {{ill|Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|ja|柳本大塚古墳}} (94 meters) are scattered.
The royal tombs following the Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group shifted from the southeastern part of the Nara Basin to the northwest, forming the {{ill|Saki Tsurugamine Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} (western group). This shift is thought to reflect the relocation of royal power.

revision.text:
'''Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (柳本古墳群, *Yanagimoto Kofungun*) is a group of early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} Kofuns located in Yanagimoto-chō, {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}. Together with the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}}, it is also referred to as the '''Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (大和・柳本古墳群). To the south lies the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纏向古墳群}}.
== Overview == This kofun group is situated on the southern ridges of hills, separated by a valley from the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}}, and comprises two large kofun and several medium- and small-sized keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳). The main mound, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun}}, is accompanied by three subordinate tumuli: {{ill|Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳}}, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun}} (120 meters), and {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|南アンド山古墳|Andon-yama Kofun=Andon-yama Kofun|lt=South Ando-yama Kofun}} (65 meters). To the south, another group centers on {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}} and includes the {{ill|Kamiyama Kofun|ja|上ノ山古墳}} (125 meters) and {{ill|Shiurou Kofun|ja|シウロウ塚古墳}} (120 meters). To the west, tumuli such as {{ill|Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|ja|柳本大塚古墳}} (94 meters) are scattered.
The royal tombs following the Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group shifted from the southeastern part of the Nara Basin to the northwest, forming the {{ill|Saki Tsurugamine Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} (western group). This shift is thought to reflect the relocation of royal power.
== Notable Kofun == [[File:Andonyama 001.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Andon-yama Kofun}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}.}]]] [[File:CG S Mukaiyama Kofun 11 7p BW.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}]]] [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|Location of the Yanagimoto Kofun Group and nearby kofun groups]]

revision.text:
'''Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (柳本古墳群, *Yanagimoto Kofungun*) is a group of early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} Kofuns located in Yanagimoto-chō, {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市|12=draft}}. Together with the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|12=draft}}, it is also referred to as the '''Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (大和・柳本古墳群). To the south lies the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纏向古墳群|12=draft}}.
== Overview == This kofun group is situated on the southern ridges of hills, separated by a valley from the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|12=draft}}, and comprises two large kofun and several medium- and small-sized keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳). The main mound, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|12=draft}}, is accompanied by three subordinate tumuli: {{ill|Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|12=draft}} (120 meters), and {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|南アンド山古墳|Andon-yama Kofun=Andon-yama Kofun|lt=South Ando-yama Kofun|12=draft}} (65 meters). To the south, another group centers on {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=draft}} and includes the {{ill|Kamiyama Kofun|ja|上ノ山古墳|12=draft}} (125 meters) and {{ill|Shiurou Kofun|ja|シウロウ塚古墳|12=draft}} (120 meters). To the west, tumuli such as {{ill|Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|ja|柳本大塚古墳|12=draft}} (94 meters) are scattered.
The royal tombs following the Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group shifted from the southeastern part of the Nara Basin to the northwest, forming the {{ill|Saki Tsurugamine Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=draft}} (western group). This shift is thought to reflect the relocation of royal power.
== Notable Kofun == [[File:Andonyama 001.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Andon-yama Kofun}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}.}]]] [[File:CG S Mukaiyama Kofun 11 7p BW.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}]]] [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|Location of the Yanagimoto Kofun Group and nearby kofun groups]]
; Keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳) * {{ill|Kurotsuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳|12=draft}} (127.5 meters) * {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|12=draft}} (242 meters, attributed to Emperor Sujin) * {{ill|Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳|12=draft}} (113 meters) * {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=draft}} (300 meters, attributed to Emperor Keikō)
; Square-shaped tumuli (双方中円墳) * {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳|12=draft}} (150 meters) == See Also == * {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|12=draft}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{citation needed|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster}} {{citation needed|Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group}} {{citation needed|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster}} {{citation needed|Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group}}
[[simple:user:immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster]]

revision.text:
'''柳本古墳群'''（やなぎもとこふんぐん）は、[[天理市]]柳本町に所在する[[古墳時代]]前期の古墳群である。[[大和古墳群]]と併せて'''大和・柳本古墳群'''とも言われる。その南に[[纏向古墳群]]が存在する。
== 概要 == 本古墳群は、[[大和古墳群]]と谷を隔てて南の丘陵の縁辺に営まれており、2基の巨大古墳とその周囲の中・小形前方後円墳からなっている。まず、行燈山古墳を主墳に大和天神山・アンド山（120メートル）・南アンド山（65メートル）の3古墳が従属している。また南に渋谷古墳を主墳に上ノ山（125メートル）・シウロウ塚（120メートル）の二つの古墳群と西の方に柳本大塚（94メートル）などの古墳が点在している。
大和・柳本古墳群に続く王墓は、奈良盆地の東南の地を離れ、盆地西北部の地に[[佐紀盾列古墳群]]（西群）として営まれる。王権が移動したと考えられている。
== 主な古墳 == [[File:Andonyama 001.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|行燈山古墳}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}。}}]] [[File:CG S Mukaiyama Kofun 11 7p BW.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|渋谷向山古墳}}]] [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|柳本古墳群と他の古墳群の位置関係]] ;前方後円墳 *[[黒塚古墳]]（127.5メートル） *[[行燈山古墳]]（242メートル、伝崇神天皇陵） *[[大和天神山古墳]]（113メートル） *[[渋谷向山古墳]]（300メートル、伝景行天皇陵） ;双方中円墳 *[[櫛山古墳]]（150メートル）
== 関連項目 == *[[大和古墳群]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やなきもとこふんくん}} [[Category:柳本古墳群|*]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:古墳群]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:柳本古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tenri|qid=Q502196}}|part_of={{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|qid=Q11626169}}; {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|qid=Q1793171}}; {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|qid=Q11544664}}; {{ill|Shibutani Mukaiyama Kofun|qid=Q11561706}}; {{ill|Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|qid=Q11433432}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11534332]] Wikidata name: Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (柳本古墳群, *Yanagimoto Kofungun*) is a group of early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] Kofuns located in Yanagimoto-chō, [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara Prefecture]]. Together with the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, it is also referred to as the '''Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (大和・柳本古墳群). To the south lies the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纏向古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=纒向古墳群}}.
== Overview == This kofun group is situated on the southern ridges of hills, separated by a valley from the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and comprises two large kofun and several medium- and small-sized keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳). The main mound, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}}, is accompanied by three subordinate tumuli: {{ill|Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}} (120 meters), and {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|南アンド山古墳|Andon-yama Kofun=Andon-yama Kofun|lt=South Ando-yama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=行燈山古墳}} (65 meters). To the south, another group centers on {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} and includes the {{ill|Kamiyama Kofun|ja|上ノ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (125 meters) and {{ill|Shiurou Kofun|ja|シウロウ塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiurou Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (120 meters). To the west, tumuli such as {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|ja|柳本大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|qq=}} (94 meters) are scattered.
The royal tombs following the Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group shifted from the southeastern part of the Nara Basin to the northwest, forming the {{ill|Saki Tsurugamine Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tsurugamine Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} (western group). This shift is thought to reflect the relocation of royal power.
== Notable Kofun == [[File:Andonyama 001.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Andon-yama Kofun}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}.}]]] [[File:CG S Mukaiyama Kofun 11 7p BW.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}]]] [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|Location of the Yanagimoto Kofun Group and nearby kofun groups]]
; Keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳) * [[:en:Kurozuka Kofun|Kurotsuka Kofun]] (127.5 meters) * {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}} (242 meters, attributed to Emperor Sujin) * {{ill|Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}} (113 meters) * {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} (300 meters, attributed to Emperor Keikō)
; Square-shaped tumuli (双方中円墳) * [[:en:Kushiyama Kofun|Kushiyama Kofun]] (150 meters) == See Also == * {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:柳本古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}}|part_of={{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|WD=Q11626169}}; {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|WD=Q1793171}}; {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|WD=Q11544664}}; {{ill|Shibutani Mukaiyama Kofun|WD=Q11561706}}; {{ill|Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|WD=Q11433432}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11534332]] Wikidata name: Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (柳本古墳群, *Yanagimoto Kofungun*) is a group of early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] Kofuns located in Yanagimoto-chō, [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara Prefecture]]. Together with the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, it is also referred to as the '''Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (大和・柳本古墳群). To the south lies the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纏向古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=纒向古墳群}}.
== Overview == This kofun group is situated on the southern ridges of hills, separated by a valley from the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and comprises two large kofun and several medium- and small-sized keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳). The main mound, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}}, is accompanied by three subordinate tumuli: {{ill|Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}} (120 meters), and {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|南アンド山古墳|Andon-yama Kofun=Andon-yama Kofun|lt=South Ando-yama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=行燈山古墳}} (65 meters). To the south, another group centers on {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} and includes the {{ill|Kamiyama Kofun|ja|上ノ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (125 meters) and {{ill|Shiurou Kofun|ja|シウロウ塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiurou Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (120 meters). To the west, tumuli such as {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|ja|柳本大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|qq=}} (94 meters) are scattered.
The royal tombs following the Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group shifted from the southeastern part of the Nara Basin to the northwest, forming the {{ill|Saki Tsurugamine Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tsurugamine Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} (western group). This shift is thought to reflect the relocation of royal power.
== Notable Kofun == [[File:Andonyama 001.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Andon-yama Kofun}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}.}]]] [[File:CG S Mukaiyama Kofun 11 7p BW.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}]]] [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|Location of the Yanagimoto Kofun Group and nearby kofun groups]]
; Keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳) * [[:en:Kurozuka Kofun|Kurotsuka Kofun]] (127.5 meters) * {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}} (242 meters, attributed to Emperor Sujin) * {{ill|Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}} (113 meters) * {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} (300 meters, attributed to Emperor Keikō)
; Square-shaped tumuli (双方中円墳) * [[:en:Kushiyama Kofun|Kushiyama Kofun]] (150 meters) == See Also == * {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}

Together with the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, it is also referred to as the '''Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (大和・柳本古墳群). == Overview == This kofun group is situated on the southern ridges of hills, separated by a valley from the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and comprises two large kofun and several medium- and small-sized keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳). To the south, another group centers on {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} and includes the {{ill|Kamiyama Kofun|ja|上ノ山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (125 meters) and {{ill|Shiurou Kofun|ja|シウロウ塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiurou Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (120 meters). To the west, tumuli such as {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|ja|柳本大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|qq=}} (94 meters) are scattered.

This shift is thought to reflect the relocation of royal power.

To the south, another group centers on {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} and includes the {{ill|Kamiyama Kofun|ja|上ノ山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (125 meters) and {{ill|Shiurou Kofun|ja|シウロウ塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiurou Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (120 meters). To the west, tumuli such as {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|ja|柳本大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|qq=}} (94 meters) are scattered.
The royal tombs following the Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group shifted from the southeastern part of the Nara Basin to the northwest, forming the {{ill|Saki Tsurugamine Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tsurugamine Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} (western group). This shift is thought to reflect the relocation of royal power.
== Notable Kofun == [[File:Andonyama 001.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Andon-yama Kofun}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}.}]]] [[File:CG S Mukaiyama Kofun 11 7p BW.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}]]] [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|Location of the Yanagimoto Kofun Group and nearby kofun groups]]
; Keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳) * [[:en:Kurozuka Kofun|Kurotsuka Kofun]] (127.5 meters) * {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}} (242 meters, attributed to Emperor Sujin) * {{ill|Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}} (113 meters) * {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} (300 meters, attributed to Emperor Keikō)
; Square-shaped tumuli (双方中円墳) * [[:en:Kushiyama Kofun|Kushiyama Kofun]] (150 meters) == See Also == * {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:柳本古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}}|part_of={{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|WD=Q11626169}}; {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|WD=Q1793171}}; {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|WD=Q11544664}}; {{ill|Shibutani Mukaiyama Kofun|WD=Q11561706}}; {{ill|Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|WD=Q11433432}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11534332]] Wikidata name: Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (柳本古墳群, *Yanagimoto Kofungun*) is a group of early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] Kofuns located in Yanagimoto-chō, [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara Prefecture]]. Together with the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, it is also referred to as the '''Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (大和・柳本古墳群). To the south lies the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纏向古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Group|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=纒向古墳群}}.
== Overview == This kofun group is situated on the southern ridges of hills, separated by a valley from the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and comprises two large kofun and several medium- and small-sized keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳). The main mound, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}}, is accompanied by three subordinate tumuli: {{ill|Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}} (120 meters), and {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|南アンド山古墳|Andon-yama Kofun=Andon-yama Kofun|lt=South Ando-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=行燈山古墳}} (65 meters). To the south, another group centers on {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} and includes the {{ill|Kamiyama Kofun|ja|上ノ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (125 meters) and {{ill|Shiurou Kofun|ja|シウロウ塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiurou Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (120 meters). To the west, tumuli such as {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|ja|柳本大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|qq=}} (94 meters) are scattered.
The royal tombs following the Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group shifted from the southeastern part of the Nara Basin to the northwest, forming the {{ill|Saki Tsurugamine Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tsurugamine Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} (western group). This shift is thought to reflect the relocation of royal power.
== Notable Kofun == [[File:Andonyama 001.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Andon-yama Kofun}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}.}]]] [[File:CG S Mukaiyama Kofun 11 7p BW.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}]]] [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|Location of the Yanagimoto Kofun Group and nearby kofun groups]]
; Keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳) * [[:en:Kurozuka Kofun|Kurotsuka Kofun]] (127.5 meters) * {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}} (242 meters, attributed to Emperor Sujin) * {{ill|Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}} (113 meters) * {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} (300 meters, attributed to Emperor Keikō)
; Square-shaped tumuli (双方中円墳) * [[:en:Kushiyama Kofun|Kushiyama Kofun]] (150 meters) == See Also == * {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}

To the south, another group centers on {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} and includes the {{ill|Kamiyama Kofun|ja|上ノ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (125 meters) and {{ill|Shiurou Kofun|ja|シウロウ塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiurou Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (120 meters). To the west, tumuli such as {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|ja|柳本大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|qq=}} (94 meters) are scattered.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:柳本古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}}|part_of={{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|WD=Q11626169}}; {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|WD=Q1793171}}; {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|WD=Q11544664}}; {{ill|Shibutani Mukaiyama Kofun|WD=Q11561706}}; {{ill|Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|WD=Q11433432}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11534332]] Wikidata name: Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (柳本古墳群, *Yanagimoto Kofungun*) is a group of early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] Kofuns located in Yanagimoto-chō, [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara Prefecture]]. Together with the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, it is also referred to as the '''Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (大和・柳本古墳群). To the south lies the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纏向古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Group|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=纒向古墳群}}.
== Overview == This kofun group is situated on the southern ridges of hills, separated by a valley from the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and comprises two large kofun and several medium- and small-sized keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳). The main mound, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}}, is accompanied by three subordinate tumuli: {{ill|Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}} (120 meters), and {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|南アンド山古墳|Andon-yama Kofun=Andon-yama Kofun|lt=South Ando-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=行燈山古墳}} (65 meters). To the south, another group centers on {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} and includes the {{ill|Kamiyama Kofun|ja|上ノ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (125 meters) and {{ill|Shiurou Kofun|ja|シウロウ塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiurou Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (120 meters). To the west, tumuli such as {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|ja|柳本大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|qq=}} (94 meters) are scattered.
The royal tombs following the Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group shifted from the southeastern part of the Nara Basin to the northwest, forming the {{ill|Saki Tsurugamine Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tsurugamine Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} (western group). This shift is thought to reflect the relocation of royal power.
== Notable Kofun == [[File:Andonyama 001.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Andon-yama Kofun}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}.}]]] [[File:CG S Mukaiyama Kofun 11 7p BW.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}]]] [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|Location of the Yanagimoto Kofun Group and nearby kofun groups]]
; Keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳) * [[:en:Kurozuka Kofun|Kurotsuka Kofun]] (127.5 meters) * {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}} (242 meters, attributed to Emperor Sujin) * {{ill|Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}} (113 meters) * {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} (300 meters, attributed to Emperor Keikō)
; Square-shaped tumuli (双方中円墳) * [[:en:Kushiyama Kofun|Kushiyama Kofun]] (150 meters) == See Also == * {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}

To the west, tumuli such as {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|ja|柳本大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|qq=}} (94 meters) are scattered.
The royal tombs following the Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group shifted from the southeastern part of the Nara Basin to the northwest, forming the {{ill|Saki Tsurugamine Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tsurugamine Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} (western group). This shift is thought to reflect the relocation of royal power.
== Notable Kofun == [[File:Andonyama 001.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Andon-yama Kofun}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}.}]]] [[File:CG S Mukaiyama Kofun 11 7p BW.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}]]] [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|Location of the Yanagimoto Kofun Group and nearby kofun groups]]
; Keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳) * [[:en:Kurozuka Kofun|Kurotsuka Kofun]] (127.5 meters) * {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}} (242 meters, attributed to Emperor Sujin) * {{ill|Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}} (113 meters) * {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} (300 meters, attributed to Emperor Keikō)
; Square-shaped tumuli (双方中円墳) * [[:en:Kushiyama Kofun|Kushiyama Kofun]] (150 meters) == See Also == * {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:柳本古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}}|part_of={{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|WD=Q11626169}}; {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|WD=Q1793171}}; {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|WD=Q11544664}}; {{ill|Shibutani Mukaiyama Kofun|WD=Q11561706}}; {{ill|Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|WD=Q11433432}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11534332]] Wikidata name: Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (柳本古墳群, *Yanagimoto Kofungun*) is a group of early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] Kofuns located in Yanagimoto-chō, [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara Prefecture]]. Together with the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, it is also referred to as the '''Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (大和・柳本古墳群). To the south lies the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纏向古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Group|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=纒向古墳群}}.
== Overview == This kofun group is situated on the southern ridges of hills, separated by a valley from the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and comprises two large kofun and several medium- and small-sized keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳). The main mound, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}}, is accompanied by three subordinate tumuli: {{ill|Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}} (120 meters), and {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|南アンド山古墳|Andon-yama Kofun=Andon-yama Kofun|lt=South Ando-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=行燈山古墳}} (65 meters). To the south, another group centers on {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} and includes the {{ill|Kamiyama Kofun|ja|上ノ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (125 meters) and {{ill|Shiurou Kofun|ja|シウロウ塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiurou Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (120 meters). To the west, tumuli such as {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|ja|柳本大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|qq=}} (94 meters) are scattered.
The royal tombs following the Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group shifted from the southeastern part of the Nara Basin to the northwest, forming the {{ill|Saki Tsurugamine Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tsurugamine Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} (western group). This shift is thought to reflect the relocation of royal power.
== Notable Kofun == [[File:Andonyama 001.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Andon-yama Kofun}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}.}]]] [[File:CG S Mukaiyama Kofun 11 7p BW.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}]]] [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|Location of the Yanagimoto Kofun Group and nearby kofun groups]]
; Keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳) * [[:en:Kurozuka Kofun|Kurotsuka Kofun]] (127.5 meters) * {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}} (242 meters, attributed to Emperor Sujin) * {{ill|Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}} (113 meters) * {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} (300 meters, attributed to Emperor Keikō)
; Square-shaped tumuli (双方中円墳) * [[:en:Kushiyama Kofun|Kushiyama Kofun]] (150 meters) == See Also == * {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}

To the south, another group centers on {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} and includes the {{ill|Kamiyama Kofun|ja|上ノ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (125 meters) and {{ill|Shiurou Kofun|ja|シウロウ塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiurou Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (120 meters). ; Keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳) * [[:en:Kurozuka Kofun|Kurotsuka Kofun]] (127.5 meters) * {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}} (242 meters, attributed to Emperor Sujin) * {{ill|Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}} (113 meters) * {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} (300 meters, attributed to Emperor Keikō)

revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]" to ""
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:柳本古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}}|part_of={{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|WD=Q11626169}}; {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|WD=Q1793171}}; {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|WD=Q11544664}}; {{ill|Shibutani Mukaiyama Kofun|WD=Q11561706}}; {{ill|Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|WD=Q11433432}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11534332]] Wikidata name: Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (柳本古墳群, *Yanagimoto Kofungun*) is a group of early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] Kofuns located in Yanagimoto-chō, [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara Prefecture]]. Together with the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, it is also referred to as the '''Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (大和・柳本古墳群). To the south lies the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纏向古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Group|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=纒向古墳群}}.
== Overview == This kofun group is situated on the southern ridges of hills, separated by a valley from the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and comprises two large kofun and several medium- and small-sized keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳). The main mound, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}}, is accompanied by three subordinate tumuli: {{ill|Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}} (120 meters), and {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|南アンド山古墳|Andon-yama Kofun=Andon-yama Kofun|lt=South Ando-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=行燈山古墳}} (65 meters). To the south, another group centers on {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} and includes the {{ill|Kamiyama Kofun|ja|上ノ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (125 meters) and {{ill|Shiurou Kofun|ja|シウロウ塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiurou Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (120 meters). To the west, tumuli such as {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|ja|柳本大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|qq=}} (94 meters) are scattered.
The royal tombs following the Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group shifted from the southeastern part of the Nara Basin to the northwest, forming the {{ill|Saki Tsurugamine Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tsurugamine Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} (western group). This shift is thought to reflect the relocation of royal power.
== Notable Kofun == [[File:Andonyama 001.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Andon-yama Kofun}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}.}]]] [[File:CG S Mukaiyama Kofun 11 7p BW.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}]]] [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|Location of the Yanagimoto Kofun Group and nearby kofun groups]]
; Keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳) * [[:en:Kurozuka Kofun|Kurotsuka Kofun]] (127.5 meters) * {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}} (242 meters, attributed to Emperor Sujin) * {{ill|Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}} (113 meters) * {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} (300 meters, attributed to Emperor Keikō)
; Square-shaped tumuli (双方中円墳) * [[:en:Kushiyama Kofun|Kushiyama Kofun]] (150 meters) == See Also == * {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}

To the south, another group centers on {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} and includes the {{ill|Kamiyama Kofun|ja|上ノ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (125 meters) and {{ill|Shiurou Kofun|ja|シウロウ塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiurou Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (120 meters).
