revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:柳本古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:柳本古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}}|part_of={{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|WD=Q11626169}}; {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|WD=Q1793171}}; {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|WD=Q11544664}}; {{ill|Shibutani Mukaiyama Kofun|WD=Q11561706}}; {{ill|Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|WD=Q11433432}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11534332]] Wikidata name: Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (柳本古墳群, *Yanagimoto Kofungun*) is a group of early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] Kofuns located in Yanagimoto-chō, [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara Prefecture]]. Together with the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, it is also referred to as the '''Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (大和・柳本古墳群). To the south lies the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纏向古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Group|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=纒向古墳群}}.
== Overview == This kofun group is situated on the southern ridges of hills, separated by a valley from the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and comprises two large kofun and several medium- and small-sized keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳). The main mound, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}}, is accompanied by three subordinate tumuli: {{ill|Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}} (120 meters), and {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|南アンド山古墳|Andon-yama Kofun=Andon-yama Kofun|lt=South Ando-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=行燈山古墳}} (65 meters). To the south, another group centers on {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} and includes the {{ill|Kamiyama Kofun|ja|上ノ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (125 meters) and {{ill|Shiurou Kofun|ja|シウロウ塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiurou Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (120 meters). To the west, tumuli such as {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|ja|柳本大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|qq=}} (94 meters) are scattered.
The royal tombs following the Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group shifted from the southeastern part of the Nara Basin to the northwest, forming the {{ill|Saki Tsurugamine Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tsurugamine Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} (western group). This shift is thought to reflect the relocation of royal power.
== Notable Kofun == [[File:Andonyama 001.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Andon-yama Kofun}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}.}]]] [[File:CG S Mukaiyama Kofun 11 7p BW.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}]]] [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|Location of the Yanagimoto Kofun Group and nearby kofun groups]]
; Keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳) * [[:en:Kurozuka Kofun|Kurotsuka Kofun]] (127.5 meters) * {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}} (242 meters, attributed to Emperor Sujin) * {{ill|Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}} (113 meters) * {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} (300 meters, attributed to Emperor Keikō)
; Square-shaped tumuli (双方中円墳) * [[:en:Kushiyama Kofun|Kushiyama Kofun]] (150 meters) == See Also == * {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}

The main mound, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}}, is accompanied by three subordinate tumuli: {{ill|Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}} (120 meters), and {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|南アンド山古墳|Andon-yama Kofun=Andon-yama Kofun|lt=South Ando-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=行燈山古墳}} (65 meters). To the south, another group centers on {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} and includes the {{ill|Kamiyama Kofun|ja|上ノ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (125 meters) and {{ill|Shiurou Kofun|ja|シウロウ塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiurou Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (120 meters). To the west, tumuli such as {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|ja|柳本大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|qq=}} (94 meters) are scattered.
The royal tombs following the Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group shifted from the southeastern part of the Nara Basin to the northwest, forming the {{ill|Saki Tsurugamine Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tsurugamine Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} (western group). This shift is thought to reflect the relocation of royal power.
== Notable Kofun == [[File:Andonyama 001.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Andon-yama Kofun}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}.}]]] [[File:CG S Mukaiyama Kofun 11 7p BW.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}]]] [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|Location of the Yanagimoto Kofun Group and nearby kofun groups]]
; Keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳) * [[:en:Kurozuka Kofun|Kurotsuka Kofun]] (127.5 meters) * {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}} (242 meters, attributed to Emperor Sujin) * {{ill|Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}} (113 meters) * {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} (300 meters, attributed to Emperor Keikō)
; Square-shaped tumuli (双方中円墳) * [[:en:Kushiyama Kofun|Kushiyama Kofun]] (150 meters) == See Also == * {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:柳本古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:柳本古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:柳本古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}}|part_of={{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|WD=Q11626169}}; {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|WD=Q1793171}}; {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|WD=Q11544664}}; {{ill|Shibutani Mukaiyama Kofun|WD=Q11561706}}; {{ill|Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|WD=Q11433432}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11534332]] Wikidata name: Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (柳本古墳群, *Yanagimoto Kofungun*) is a group of early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] Kofuns located in Yanagimoto-chō, [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara Prefecture]]. Together with the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, it is also referred to as the '''Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (大和・柳本古墳群). To the south lies the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纏向古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Group|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=纒向古墳群}}.

To the south, another group centers on {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} and includes the {{ill|Kamiyama Kofun|ja|上ノ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (125 meters) and {{ill|Shiurou Kofun|ja|シウロウ塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiurou Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (120 meters). To the west, tumuli such as {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|ja|柳本大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|qq=}} (94 meters) are scattered.
The royal tombs following the Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group shifted from the southeastern part of the Nara Basin to the northwest, forming the {{ill|Saki Tsurugamine Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tsurugamine Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} (western group). This shift is thought to reflect the relocation of royal power.

; Keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳) * [[:en:Kurozuka Kofun|Kurotsuka Kofun]] (127.5 meters) * {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}} (242 meters, attributed to Emperor Sujin) * {{ill|Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}} (113 meters) * {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} (300 meters, attributed to Emperor Keikō)

; Keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳) * [[:en:Kurozuka Kofun|Kurotsuka Kofun]] (127.5 meters) * {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}} (242 meters, attributed to Emperor Sujin) * {{ill|Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}} (113 meters) * {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} (300 meters, attributed to Emperor Keikō)

revision.comment: Moving from [[Category:Pages with conflicting qids]] to [[Category:Qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:柳本古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:柳本古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:柳本古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}}|part_of={{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|WD=Q11626169}}; {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|WD=Q1793171}}; {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|WD=Q11544664}}; {{ill|Shibutani Mukaiyama Kofun|WD=Q11561706}}; {{ill|Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|WD=Q11433432}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11534332]] Wikidata name: Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (柳本古墳群, *Yanagimoto Kofungun*) is a group of early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] Kofuns located in Yanagimoto-chō, [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara Prefecture]]. Together with the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, it is also referred to as the '''Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (大和・柳本古墳群). To the south lies the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纏向古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Group|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=纒向古墳群}}.
== Overview == This kofun group is situated on the southern ridges of hills, separated by a valley from the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and comprises two large kofun and several medium- and small-sized keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳). The main mound, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}}, is accompanied by three subordinate tumuli: {{ill|Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}} (120 meters), and {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|南アンド山古墳|Andon-yama Kofun=Andon-yama Kofun|lt=South Ando-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=行燈山古墳}} (65 meters). To the south, another group centers on {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} and includes the {{ill|Kamiyama Kofun|ja|上ノ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (125 meters) and {{ill|Shiurou Kofun|ja|シウロウ塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiurou Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (120 meters). To the west, tumuli such as {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|ja|柳本大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|qq=}} (94 meters) are scattered.
The royal tombs following the Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group shifted from the southeastern part of the Nara Basin to the northwest, forming the {{ill|Saki Tsurugamine Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tsurugamine Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} (western group). This shift is thought to reflect the relocation of royal power.
== Notable Kofun == [[File:Andonyama 001.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Andon-yama Kofun}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}.}]]] [[File:CG S Mukaiyama Kofun 11 7p BW.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}]]] [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|Location of the Yanagimoto Kofun Group and nearby kofun groups]]
; Keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳) * [[:en:Kurozuka Kofun|Kurotsuka Kofun]] (127.5 meters) * {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}} (242 meters, attributed to Emperor Sujin) * {{ill|Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}} (113 meters) * {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} (300 meters, attributed to Emperor Keikō)
; Square-shaped tumuli (双方中円墳) * [[:en:Kushiyama Kofun|Kushiyama Kofun]] (150 meters) == See Also == * {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:柳本古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:柳本古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:柳本古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}}|part_of={{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|WD=Q11626169}}; {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|WD=Q1793171}}; {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|WD=Q11544664}}; {{ill|Shibutani Mukaiyama Kofun|WD=Q11561706}}; {{ill|Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|WD=Q11433432}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11534332]] Wikidata name: Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (柳本古墳群, *Yanagimoto Kofungun*) is a group of early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} Kofuns located in Yanagimoto-chō, {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri, Nara Prefecture}}. Together with the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, it is also referred to as the '''Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (大和・柳本古墳群). To the south lies the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纏向古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Group|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=纒向古墳群}}.
== Overview == This kofun group is situated on the southern ridges of hills, separated by a valley from the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and comprises two large kofun and several medium- and small-sized keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳). The main mound, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}}, is accompanied by three subordinate tumuli: {{ill|Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}} (120 meters), and {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|南アンド山古墳|Andon-yama Kofun=Andon-yama Kofun|lt=South Ando-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=行燈山古墳}} (65 meters). To the south, another group centers on {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} and includes the {{ill|Kamiyama Kofun|ja|上ノ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (125 meters) and {{ill|Shiurou Kofun|ja|シウロウ塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiurou Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (120 meters). To the west, tumuli such as {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|ja|柳本大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|qq=}} (94 meters) are scattered.
The royal tombs following the Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group shifted from the southeastern part of the Nara Basin to the northwest, forming the {{ill|Saki Tsurugamine Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tsurugamine Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} (western group). This shift is thought to reflect the relocation of royal power.
== Notable Kofun == [[File:Andonyama 001.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Andon-yama Kofun}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}.}]]] [[File:CG S Mukaiyama Kofun 11 7p BW.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}]]] [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|Location of the Yanagimoto Kofun Group and nearby kofun groups]]
; Keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳) * {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|en|Kurozuka Kofun|WD=Q1793171|lt=Kurotsuka Kofun}} (127.5 meters) * {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}} (242 meters, attributed to Emperor Sujin) * {{ill|Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}} (113 meters) * {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} (300 meters, attributed to Emperor Keikō)
; Square-shaped tumuli (双方中円墳) * {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|en|Kushiyama Kofun|WD=Q11544664|lt=Kushiyama Kofun}} (150 meters) == See Also == * {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}

Together with the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, it is also referred to as the '''Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (大和・柳本古墳群). To the south lies the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纏向古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Group|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=纒向古墳群}}.

To the south, another group centers on {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} and includes the {{ill|Kamiyama Kofun|ja|上ノ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (125 meters) and {{ill|Shiurou Kofun|ja|シウロウ塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiurou Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (120 meters). To the west, tumuli such as {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|ja|柳本大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|qq=}} (94 meters) are scattered.

This shift is thought to reflect the relocation of royal power.

; Keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳) * {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|en|Kurozuka Kofun|WD=Q1793171|lt=Kurotsuka Kofun}} (127.5 meters) * {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}} (242 meters, attributed to Emperor Sujin) * {{ill|Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}} (113 meters) * {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} (300 meters, attributed to Emperor Keikō)

== Overview == This kofun group is situated on the southern ridges of hills, separated by a valley from the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and comprises two large kofun and several medium- and small-sized keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳). The main mound, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}}, is accompanied by three subordinate tumuli: {{ill|Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}} (120 meters), and {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|南アンド山古墳|Andon-yama Kofun=Andon-yama Kofun|lt=South Ando-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=行燈山古墳}} (65 meters). To the south, another group centers on {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} and includes the {{ill|Kamiyama Kofun|ja|上ノ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (125 meters) and {{ill|Shiurou Kofun|ja|シウロウ塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiurou Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (120 meters). To the west, tumuli such as {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|ja|柳本大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|qq=}} (94 meters) are scattered.
The royal tombs following the Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group shifted from the southeastern part of the Nara Basin to the northwest, forming the {{ill|Saki Tsurugamine Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tsurugamine Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} (western group). This shift is thought to reflect the relocation of royal power.
== Notable Kofun == [[File:Andonyama 001.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Andon-yama Kofun}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}.}]]] [[File:CG S Mukaiyama Kofun 11 7p BW.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}]]] [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|Location of the Yanagimoto Kofun Group and nearby kofun groups]]
; Keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳) * {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|en|Kurozuka Kofun|WD=Q1793171|lt=Kurotsuka Kofun}} (127.5 meters) * {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}} (242 meters, attributed to Emperor Sujin) * {{ill|Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}} (113 meters) * {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} (300 meters, attributed to Emperor Keikō)
; Square-shaped tumuli (双方中円墳) * {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|en|Kushiyama Kofun|WD=Q11544664|lt=Kushiyama Kofun}} (150 meters) == See Also == * {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:柳本古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:柳本古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:柳本古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}}|part_of={{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|WD=Q11626169}}; {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|WD=Q1793171}}; {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|WD=Q11544664}}; {{ill|Shibutani Mukaiyama Kofun|WD=Q11561706}}; {{ill|Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|WD=Q11433432}}}} '''Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (柳本古墳群, *Yanagimoto Kofungun*) is a group of early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} Kofuns located in Yanagimoto-chō, {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri, Nara Prefecture}}. Together with the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, it is also referred to as the '''Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (大和・柳本古墳群). To the south lies the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纏向古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Group|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=纒向古墳群}}.
== Overview == This kofun group is situated on the southern ridges of hills, separated by a valley from the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and comprises two large kofun and several medium- and small-sized keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳). To the south, another group centers on {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} and includes the {{ill|Kamiyama Kofun|ja|上ノ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (125 meters) and {{ill|Shiurou Kofun|ja|シウロウ塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiurou Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (120 meters). To the west, tumuli such as {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|ja|柳本大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|qq=}} (94 meters) are scattered.
The royal tombs following the Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group shifted from the southeastern part of the Nara Basin to the northwest, forming the {{ill|Saki Tsurugamine Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tsurugamine Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} (western group). This shift is thought to reflect the relocation of royal power.

; Keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳) * {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|en|Kurozuka Kofun|WD=Q1793171|lt=Kurotsuka Kofun|1=Kurozuka Kofun}} (127.5 meters) * {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}} (242 meters, attributed to Emperor Sujin) * {{ill|Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}} (113 meters) * {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} (300 meters, attributed to Emperor Keikō)
; Square-shaped tumuli (双方中円墳) * {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|en|Kushiyama Kofun|WD=Q11544664|lt=Kushiyama Kofun|1=Kushiyama Kofun}} (150 meters) == See Also == * {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Pages]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:柳本古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:柳本古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:柳本古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}}|part_of={{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|WD=Q11626169|1=Andon-yama Kofun}}; {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|WD=Q1793171|1=Kurozuka Kofun}}; {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|WD=Q11544664|1=Kushiyama Kofun}}; {{ill|Shibutani Mukaiyama Kofun|WD=Q11561706|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}}; {{ill|Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|WD=Q11433432|1=Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun}}}} '''Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (柳本古墳群, *Yanagimoto Kofungun*) is a group of early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} Kofuns located in Yanagimoto-chō, {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri, Nara Prefecture|1=Tenri, Nara}}. Together with the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, it is also referred to as the '''Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group''' (大和・柳本古墳群). To the south lies the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Cluster|lt=Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纏向古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Group|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=纒向古墳群}}.
== Overview == This kofun group is situated on the southern ridges of hills, separated by a valley from the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and comprises two large kofun and several medium- and small-sized keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳). The main mound, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}}, is accompanied by three subordinate tumuli: {{ill|Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}} (120 meters), and {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|南アンド山古墳|Andon-yama Kofun=Andon-yama Kofun|lt=South Ando-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=行燈山古墳}} (65 meters). To the south, another group centers on {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} and includes the {{ill|Kamiyama Kofun|ja|上ノ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (125 meters) and {{ill|Shiurou Kofun|ja|シウロウ塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiurou Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (120 meters). To the west, tumuli such as {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|ja|柳本大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Otsuka Kofun|qq=}} (94 meters) are scattered.
The royal tombs following the Yamato-Yanagimoto Kofun Group shifted from the southeastern part of the Nara Basin to the northwest, forming the {{ill|Saki Tsurugamine Kofun Group|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tsurugamine Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} (western group). This shift is thought to reflect the relocation of royal power.
== Notable Kofun == [[File:Andonyama 001.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Andon-yama Kofun}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}.}]]] [[File:CG S Mukaiyama Kofun 11 7p BW.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{center|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun}]]] [[File:Yodo River and Yamato River Basins.svg|300px|thumb|right|Location of the Yanagimoto Kofun Group and nearby kofun groups]]
; Keyhole-shaped tumuli (前方後円墳) * {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|en|Kurozuka Kofun|WD=Q1793171|lt=Kurotsuka Kofun|1=Kurozuka Kofun}} (127.5 meters) * {{ill|Andon-yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}} (242 meters, attributed to Emperor Sujin) * {{ill|Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjin'yama Kofun|lt=Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}} (113 meters) * {{ill|Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|qq=}} (300 meters, attributed to Emperor Keikō)
; Square-shaped tumuli (双方中円墳) * {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|en|Kushiyama Kofun|WD=Q11544664|lt=Kushiyama Kofun|1=Kushiyama Kofun}} (150 meters) == See Also == * {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}
